title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
3 Goals Where Endowment Policy Fits Perfectly
Meta description: An endowment policy is designed to fulfil a wide range of goals and requirements. Check out three goals that such a plan enables you to satisfy.
Keywords: endowment policy, endowment plan, endowment insurance
In India, you can find multiple types of life insurance policies, each satisfying a different set of needs and requirements. One of the most popular types of life insurance plans is an endowment policy.
Although such an insurance plan is more of a traditional investment option, it is perfect for satisfying a wide range of goals. In this article, we’re going to take a look at three goals that an endowment plan is designed to satisfy. But before we proceed, let’s first try to get to know the plan.
Life Insurance Endowment Policies
As you’ve already seen above, an endowment plan is essentially a type of life insurance that combines two aspects - insurance and savings. Such a plan provides you with a life cover of a certain predetermined sum of money for a specified period of time, in exchange for premiums.
Additionally, it also provides you with guaranteed returns, in the form of maturity benefits, at the end of the policy tenure as well. Since endowment plans provide both death and maturity benefits, they’re quite popular amongst insurance buyers.
Types of Endowment Policies
When it comes to endowment policies, there are multiple types - full-endowment, low-cost endowment, unitised with-profit endowment, non-profit endowment. Here’s a closer look at these four types of insurance plans.
Full-Endowment
A full endowment policy is designed to provide the policyholder with a fixed sum of money upon their survival till the end of the policy tenure. Additionally, the policyholder will also be entitled to receive bonuses and other loyalty additions on maturity as well. Such an insurance plan generally provides a return that’s usually higher than what was promised at the time of purchase.
Low-Cost Endowment
As the name itself suggests, a low-cost endowment plan levies low premiums. However, the returns that you get from such a plan also usually tend to be low. Low-cost endowment policies may be the right option for conservative investors who put capital preservation above returns.
Unitised Full Endowment
In a unitised full endowment plan, the premiums are invested in the equity market. And depending on the performance of the market, you stand a chance to gain high returns on your investment. In addition to that, such plans also come with an aspect of guaranteed returns, designed to protect you from the volatility of the equity market. So in a way, unitised full endowment plans eliminate risk and give you the chance to earn higher returns.
Non-Profit Endowment
With non-profit endowment plans, the policyholder only gets the guaranteed sum assured under the plan. Bonuses or other benefits such as loyalty additions are not paid out at the end of the tenure.
3 Goals That You Can Fulfil With an Endowment Insurance Plan
So, now that you’re aware of what an endowment policy is and its different types, let’s move on to the kind of goals that you can satisfy with one.
Debt-Free Life
Every individual wants to live a debt-free life. And an endowment plan is one of the best ways to ensure that. The maturity benefits that you receive at the end of the plan’s tenure can be used by you to settle your outstanding debts and loan obligations. This can free up your finances and enable you to live a life without debts. That’s not all. In the event of the policyholder’s demise, the death benefit can also be used by their nominees to settle any outstanding debts that the deceased may have left behind.
Secure Your Family’s Future
This is another major goal that almost all individuals have - ensuring their family’s financial well-being when they’re no longer around to support them. The death benefit payout that an endowment plan provides can help a policyholder financially secure their family. The payout can be used by the deceased individual’s family to take care of their expenses and further their life goals, whatever they may be.
Ensure a Stable Post-Retirement Life
Once you’re retired, you generally lose your main source of income. Therefore, it is important to ensure that you set your finances right in order to enjoy your post-retirement life without having to worry about money.
The maturity benefit of an endowment policy can help you build a large enough corpus to make sure that your post-retirement life goes on as smoothly as possible without having to worry about finances. For instance, you could invest the lump sum amount that you receive upon the maturity of an endowment plan in an annuity plan and receive a monthly income, which can be used to take care of your family’s expenses.
Conclusion
As you can clearly see, an endowment insurance plan is an effective investment option that’s designed to ensure financial freedom for not only yourself, but also your family. That said, before you go ahead and purchase an endowment policy for yourself, always remember to evaluate it thoroughly to determine whether it fits with your goals perfectly. This way, you can make an informed purchase decision.
